facebook.content:
"But he gives us more grace. That is why Scripture says: 'God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble.'"

This verse reveals the authority structure of the Kingdom. Jesus is the head, we are the body. But instead of exercising our natural pride and self-importance, we must submit to His humility and authority. When we do, He gives us more grace to live in the fullness of the Kingdom.
facebook.fullPost:
"But he gives us more grace. That is why Scripture says: 'God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble.'"

instagram.content:
But we have this treasure in jars of clay to show that this all-surpassing power is from God and is not from us. We are hard-pressed on every side, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair; persecuted, but not abandoned; struck down, but not destroyed. 2 Corinthians 4:7-9

The Kingdom doesn't rise through ease or convenience – it flourishes through the weakness of its vessels.

tiktok.content:
1 Corinthians 4:20 - The kingdom of God is not in word, but in power.

The power of God is not about speaking loudly, but about living authentically under Jesus' lordship. It's time to walk in the authority of the Kingdom, not just talk about it.
